seoTitle: Link to an Anchor in Confluence (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Learn how to link to an anchor in Confluence in 2026, from placing the anchor macro to connecting same-page and cross-page jump links.
intro: Making readers scroll through long manuals to find one paragraph wastes their time. This guide shows how to create anchor links in Confluence in 2026 so you can jump straight to a specific section, on the same page or a different one.

steps.heading: Place the anchor
steps.body: In edit mode, click your target destination line and type `/anchor` to deploy a section anchor.

steps.heading: Name the anchor
steps.body: Open the macro panel and assign a unique, single-word identifier tag, avoiding spaces or special characters.

steps.heading: Highlight your link text
steps.body: Move to your starting text block, highlight the text you want to turn into a link, and press Ctrl+K (or Cmd+K on Mac).

steps.heading: Link on the same page
steps.body: Type a hashtag followed by your exact anchor ID, for example #milestones.

steps.heading: Link across pages
steps.body: For a different page, paste its full URL and append your anchor's hashtag ID to the end.

steps.heading: Test and publish
steps.body: Confirm the jump link works correctly, then click Publish.

faq.question: Can I link to an anchor on a completely different Confluence page?
faq.answer: Yes, append your anchor's hashtag ID to the end of the target page's full URL to jump straight to that section.

faq.question: What characters should I avoid in an anchor name?
faq.answer: Keep anchor identifiers to a single word without spaces or special characters to maintain link integrity.

faq.question: What's the keyboard shortcut for adding a link?
faq.answer: Highlight your text and press Ctrl+K on Windows or Cmd+K on Mac to open the link input bar.

faq.question: How do I confirm my anchor link actually works?
faq.answer: Test the jump link in preview mode to confirm the page scrolls to the correct target line before publishing.
